title: Additive manufacturing of functionally gradient degradable tools
abstract: A method of manufacturing an article comprises depositing a metallic powder on a substrate or a worktable; fusing the metallic powder according to a preset pattern; and adjusting a composition of the metallic powder or a condition to fuse the metallic powder or a combination thereof to additively form an article such that the article has a first portion and a second portion, wherein the first portion has one or more of the following properties different than those of the second portion: corrosion rate; tensile strength; compressive strength; modulus; or hardness.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method of manufacturing an article, the method characterized by: depositing a metallic powder on a substrate or a worktable; fusing the metallic powder according to a preset pattern; and adjusting a composition of the metallic powder or a condition to fuse the metallic powder or a combination thereof to additively form an article such that the article has a first portion and a second portion, wherein the first portion has one or more of the following properties different than those of the second portion: corrosion rate; tensile strength; compressive strength; modulus; or hardness. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the depositing and the fusing are characterized by carrying out as part of a selective laser sintering process, a laser melting process, or a direct metal deposition process. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein fusing the metallic powder is further characterized by applying an energy beam from an energy source to the metallic powder. 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the metallic powder is further characterized by at least partially fused before deposited on the substrate or the worktable or fused after deposited on the substrate or the worktable. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the metallic powder is further characterized by an uncoated metallic matrix particle and a second particle; the metallic matrix particle comprising one or more of the following: a magnesium-based alloy; an aluminum-based alloy; or a zinc-based alloy; and the second particle comprising one or more of the following: a metal; an oxide of the metal; a nitride of the metal; or a cermet of the metal; wherein the metal is W; Co; Cu; Ni; or Fe. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the metallic powder is further characterized by a coated particle and optionally a second particle that is compositionally different from the coated particle; the coated particle having a particle core with a nanoscale coating disposed thereon, wherein the nanoscale coating has a melting point different than a melting point of the particle core. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the particle core is characterized by one or more of the following: Mg; Al; Zn; Mn, or an alloy thereof; and the nanoscale coating is characterized by one or more of the following: Al; Zn; Mn; Mg; Mo; W; Cu; Fe; Si; Ca; Co; Ta; Re; or Ni; an oxide thereof; a carbide thereof; or a nitride thereof; and the second particle 

 is characterized by one or more of the following: Fe; Ni; Co; W; or Cu; or oxides; nitrides; or carbides thereof. 8. The method of any one of claims 5 to 7, wherein adjusting a composition of the metallic powder is characterized by changing the relative amounts of the coated particle and the second particle or changing the relative amounts of the uncoated particle and the second particle. 9. The method of any one or more of claims 5 to 8, wherein adjusting a composition of the metallic powder is characterized by varying a particle size of the metallic matrix particle, varying a particle size of the second particle, or a combination thereof. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein adjusting a condition to fuse the metallic powder is characterized by fusing the metallic powder at an energy gradient. 11. A method of using an article, the method characterized by: forming an article according to a method of claim 1, the article comprising a first portion that is corrodible in a fluid at a first corrosion rate and a second portion that is corrodible in the fluid at a second corrosion rate, wherein the first corrosion rate is greater than the second corrosion rate; using the article to perform a first operation; exposing the article to the fluid; selectively corroding the first portion of the article to provide a corroded article; and using the corroded article to perform a second operation that is different from the first operation. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the article is characterized by a downhole article, the fluid is characterized by a wellbore fluid, and the first and second operations are characterized by wellbore operations. 13. An article characterized by a plurality of micro-sized or nano-sized galvanic cells, wherein the article has a seamless structure encompassing an empty space. 14. The article of claim 13, wherein each of the micro-sized or nano-sized galvanic cells is characterized by an anode and a cathode, the anode comprising one or more of the following: a magnesium-based alloy; an aluminum-based alloy; a zinc-based alloy; or a manganese-based alloy; and the cathode comprising a metal; an oxide of the metal; a nitride of the metal; or a cermet of the metal; wherein the metal is one or more of the following: W; Co; Cu; Ni; or Fe. 15. The article of claim 13 or claim 14, wherein the article is further characterized by a hollow firac plug, a hollow ball, or a hollow ball seat.
